rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TOMMIE N BURNS, II SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petitioner requests fees of $3,492 for his services as trustee during this two-year account period, based on his new rate of $210 per hour. 2) No waiver of account and/or bond exoneration has been sought, despite the small value of the trust corpus and income. Did Article 10, Section 4, of the trust, mentioned in the supplement, inadvertently cite Probate Code sections 2320 et seq. (governing bonds of conservators and guardians) instead of sections 2620 et seq. (governing accounts of conservators and guardians), and if so, should section 2628(a) apply to waive future accountings for this trust? See also CRC 7.903(c)(6) requiring a trustee of a court-funded trust to account in compliance with sections 2620 et seq. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE SARANTITIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/9/2023. (1) Need allegation who Frances Fabella's descendants are. Fabella's disclaimer is irrevocable and binding. It treats Fabella as if she had predeceased the decedent, thus her descendants are entitled to her interests, not Petitioner. Prob. Code sec 281. Amended Petition needs to be filed to propose the distribution of Fabella's shares to be made to her descendants, who also needs to be noticed. (2) Spousal election has not been filed. Prob. Code sec 13502. Petitioner may seek permission of late filing pursuant to LRSF 14.35.I.(5). 14.59.E. Drop-off ex-parte request may be allowed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOEL HENRY SEIGNEUR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been cleared since 5/1/2023. (1) Explanation why photocopies of an LLC information and of a trust are attached to petition. The court is not inclined to review them unless petition provides a summary and reason for review. (2) Information if there has been (or will be) any Probate proceeding in Arizona. (3) Allegation if this will is validly executed pursuant to Prob. Code [PC] sec. 6113. The will was executed in another state. (4) Confidential statement of driver's license and birth date. LRSF 14.18.E. (5) Caption and body of the petition must clarify why the proposed personal representative is "administrator with will annexed" while the will nominates petitioner to act. PC 8420; 8440. (6) Petition to list all named executors and they must be noticed properly - Paul Vincent Curran. (7) Petition to list all named beneficiaries and they must be noticed properly - Andre Seigneur (8) Petition #5.(3)-(4) and (7)-(8) to be completed re: registered domestic partner and re: issue of a predeceased child. (9) Petition #8 to list all heirs at law, including anyone who is next of kin as #6.f being affirmed. Also, Petition #5.(5) alleges that the decedent was survived by natural or adopted child; however, #8 lists stepchildren only. #5 and #6 information appears contradicting. (10) Notice (DE-121 form) to be given anyone who has not been noticed. PC 8100 (11) Notice of Remote Appearance to be given to all persons who are entitled. LRSF 14.1.D.(6). (12) Petition may address higher compensation (5%) provided by will. PC 10802. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.
